summary:
Adam showed Brad extensive progress with Alpha Claw, having built numerous apps including a command center, media gallery, recipes app, ideas generator with cron jobs, soccer training app, high school entrance exam prep, and more. He demonstrated web components for shared styling across apps, a client account management dashboard, and an ideas scoring system. Brad was impressed by the volume of work produced. They discussed technical needs including SQLite database support (which Adam got working with local storage sync), the need for server-side capabilities for secure database connections (e.g., Supabase), and app authentication/lockdown features.

Brad shared progress on Alpha Agent (the commercial wrapper around Alpha Claw) including Auth0 login, Stripe integration, Docker deployment, WhatsApp integration, and security improvements with Docker-inside-Docker sandboxing. He outlined pricing at ~3 users per $12 instance. They discussed the vision of Alpha Claw apps as prototyping tools that could be spun off into production apps. Adam also reported on his Monday conversation with Cameron - Cam cited burnout and wanting more money, but Adam gave him until Friday to come back with what he needs to turn things around, or Adam would make decisions for him. Adam noted Krista may be reading into some of Cam's messages too much but validated her core frustrations.
keyDiscussionPoints: Adam built 10+ apps with Alpha Claw in days (command center, recipes, ideas, soccer training, etc.)
Web components for shared styling across apps working well
SQLite database working with local storage sync pattern
Need for server-side gateway for secure external DB connections
Alpha Agent commercial wrapper: Auth0, Stripe, Docker, WhatsApp integration
Cameron conversation outcome: burnout cited, Friday deadline given
Krista may be over-reading some of Cam's messages but core concerns valid
PR review workflow working well with Alpha Claw
Client account management dashboard in progress
Google OAuth token refresh only lasting 1 hour - known issue
decisions: Cameron has until Friday to propose what he needs to turn things around
Brad to move Alpha Claw repo to Last Rev org for team access
App lockdown/authentication feature needed before showing to clients
Brad to focus on security fixes and Docker sandboxing

actionItems.action: Meet with Cameron on Friday to hear his proposal
Move Alpha Claw repo to Last Rev GitHub org
Fix Google OAuth token refresh (1-hour expiry issue)
Investigate server-side gateway for secure DB connections from apps

followUp: Cameron's Friday response on performance improvement plan
App authentication feature for client-facing demos
Alpha Claw repo moved to Last Rev org
